text.title: azure-nat-gateway Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for azure-nat-gateway as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: NAT Gateway has SNAT port exhaustion (~64k per public IP); high-fanout outbound workloads (web scrapers, batch HTTP) silently drop connections at the edge.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents configure a single public IP for all workloads. Add one IP per 50,000 expected concurrent connections; use a public IP prefix for large-scale egress.

failure-mode.description: Agents attach NAT Gateway to a subnet that also has a UDR pointing to Azure Firewall. Both compete for the default route; behavior is undefined.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Only one egress mechanism should control the default route (0.0.0.0/0) for a subnet. Use either NAT Gateway or Azure Firewall + UDR — not both.
